and I will measure the valley of Succoth I do not know of what nation this Succoth is, and I do not know where the Succoth is that Israel came to when they traveled from Rameses. In other commentaries I found (this is not in all editions): I will divide Shechem—I will restore to them the heritage of their father Jacob. Another explanation: And I will measure the valley of Succoth when I divide it…

Joab returned, and smote of Edom - in the valley of salt. twelve thousand. GOD, thou hast cast us off, thou hast scattered us, thou hast been displeased; oh turn thyself to us again.

“On the seventh day, prince of the children of Ephraim, Elishama son of Amihud” (Numbers 7:48). “On the seventh day, prince of the children of Ephraim, Elishama son of Amihud” – that is what is written: “Gilead is mine, and Manasseh is mine, and Ephraim is my stronghold, Judah my lawgiver” (Psalms 60:9). We learned there: Three kings and four commoners do not have a share in the World to Come.…

Joab returned, and smote of Edom - in the valley of salt. twelve thousand. GOD, thou hast cast us off, thou hast scattered us, thou hast been displeased; oh turn thyself to us again.

He went on: “Over Edom I cast my shoe”. This also had reference to a much earlier event, when Esau said to Jacob, “Make me swallow, I pray thee, some of that red stuff, and David now said, “I will stuff my shoe down his throat”.
Joab returned, and smote of Edom - in the valley of salt. twelve thousand. GOD, thou hast cast us off, thou hast scattered us, thou hast been displeased; oh turn thyself to us again.
1. O God, thou hast cast us off, thou hast scattered us, thou hast been displeased.

He went on: “Over Edom I cast my shoe”. This also had reference to a much earlier event, when Esau said to Jacob, “Make me swallow, I pray thee, some of that red stuff, and David now said, “I will stuff my shoe down his throat”.

(7) But Thou, O God, didst cast them down, and remove their seed from the earth, 9 In that there rose up against them a man that was alien to our race.
